Invariant: 'connect figures with a line'; varies: the connection semantics (e.g. Petri-net vs. class-diagram)
Invariant: the figure colors; varies: the algorithm skeleton
Both the skeleton and the steps vary freely

questions.explanation: Template Method fixes the invariant 'connect figures with a line' and varies the connection semantics via overridden hooks. (JHotDraw p.35)

questions.stem: How is Decorator used in JHotDraw, concretely?

options.text: To clone prototype figures for each tool
To compose figures into part-whole trees
To add specific visualization (borders/shadows) to a generic figure visualization at runtime

questions.explanation: Decorator adds specific visualization (borders, shadows) to generic figure visualization via DecoratorFigure. (JHotDraw p.39)

questions.stem: Composite and Decorator both wrap/forward to a Component and forward Operation(). What is the GoF-level difference?

options.text: Composite is behavioral; Decorator is creational
There is no difference at the GoF level
Composite's purpose is part-whole aggregation of many children; Decorator's purpose is adding responsibilities to one wrapped object

questions.explanation: Both are structural and forward operations, but Composite aggregates many children for part-whole hierarchies while Decorator wraps one component to add responsibilities. (JHotDraw p.19, p.36)

questions.stem: Which pairing of pattern to its JHotDraw role is INCORRECT?

options.text: Prototype -> separating layout algorithms from figures

questions.explanation: Separating layout algorithms from figures is Strategy (layouters), not Prototype. Prototype is tool-clones-figure. (JHotDraw p.28, p.46)

questions.stem: A maintainer underlines the nouns Export, Drawing, Text, Format in a request before touching any code. Which concept-location step is this, and what comes next?

options.text: Impact analysis; next, commit the baseline
Extracting domain concepts; next, locate those concepts in the code
Verification; next, write the user story

questions.explanation: Concept location begins by extracting domain concepts from the request; the extracted concepts are then located in the code. (ConceptLocation p.6; SoftwareChange p.8)

questions.stem: Which statement about the hand-off from concept location is correct?

options.text: It produces the final baseline, which Conclusion commits
It produces the user story, which Initiation prioritizes
It produces the initial impact set, which Impact Analysis then expands via dependency analysis
It produces the verification report, which testing consumes

questions.explanation: Concept location produces the initial impact set; Impact Analysis expands it by analyzing class dependencies. (SoftwareChange p.9)

questions.stem: Given the cardinality 'Drawing 1—1..n Figure', which statement is correct?

options.text: A Drawing holds exactly one Figure
A Figure holds one or more Drawings
A Drawing and a Figure are always 1—1
A Drawing aggregates one or more Figures

questions.explanation: Drawing aggregates Figures 1..n (one or more). (JHotDraw p.5)

questions.stem: Which statement about the eight-phase process is correct?

options.text: Initiation and Conclusion are world-interaction phases; Verification spans the implementation phases
Verification is the first phase, before Initiation
Concept Location is the final phase
Impact Analysis is a world-interaction phase

questions.explanation: Initiation/Conclusion are world interactions; Verification spans Prefactoring through Conclusion. (SoftwareChange p.5, p.13)

questions.stem: Best choice: a senior engineer wrote the export subsystem last week and remembers exactly where the format writers live. Which methodology locates the concept fastest here?
questions.explanation: When a programmer already knows the code, human knowledge points to the location fastest. (ConceptLocation p.7)

questions.stem: A user files: 'The app crashes when I open a .gtt file.' Which change-request FORM is this, and which maintenance category will it most likely become?

options.text: A user story, mapping to perfective maintenance
A user story, mapping to adaptive maintenance
A bug report, mapping to corrective maintenance
A bug report, mapping to preventive maintenance

questions.explanation: A crash report is a bug-report form of change request; fixing a defect is corrective maintenance. (ChangeInitiation p.4; SoftwareChange p.3)

questions.stem: You mark a class 'Next', inspect it, find the concept is in its composite but not local, then move on without changing its mark. What did you do wrong?

options.text: Nothing; Next is the correct mark after inspection
It should be marked Blank after inspection
It should be marked Unchanged because you moved on
An inspected class with the concept in its composite should be re-marked Propagating, not left as Next

questions.explanation: Next means scheduled-but-not-yet-inspected; once inspected with the concept in its composite, the class becomes Propagating. (ConceptLocation p.11)

questions.stem: You want JHotDraw figures to support an entirely new export FORMAT writer without breaking existing exports. Which design quality of JHotDraw makes this a localized change?

options.text: Its pattern-aligned, well-named classes map cleanly to domain concepts, keeping the change small
It has no class dependencies at all
It forbids adding new formats by design
It stores all formats in a single Singleton that must be rewritten

questions.explanation: JHotDraw's pattern-aligned, well-named classes map cleanly to domain concepts, which is exactly why changes stay localized. (JHotDraw p.16)

questions.stem: A junior dev says 'Factory Method and Prototype are the same — both hide which concrete class gets made.' What is the key difference per the deck?

options.text: Factory Method lets a subclass decide the class to instantiate; Prototype creates objects by cloning an existing prototypical instance
Both clone an instance with no subclassing involved
Both require a parallel factory hierarchy

questions.explanation: Factory Method defers instantiation to subclasses; Prototype copies a prototypical instance (and avoids a parallel factory hierarchy). (JHotDraw p.41, p.45)

questions.stem: In JHotDraw's MVC, after the model changes, which direction does the notification flow?

options.text: View notifies the Model, which then updates
Controller notifies the Handle, which then updates
Model notifies the View, which then updates
The Tool notifies the Drawing directly

questions.explanation: After the Controller updates the Model, the Model notifies the View, which updates. (JHotDraw p.17)

questions.stem: Which is the BEST first move when a change request mentions a concept you have never seen in this codebase and you cannot run the program?

options.text: Skip concept location and start Actualization
Reason from the concept's intension to recognize candidate code, then run a static search

questions.explanation: When the search name may differ from code names, reason from intension (meaning) to extension (instances), then apply static search. (ConceptLocation p.4-5, p.7)

questions.stem: Compare two JHotDraw patterns: one makes a tool behave differently per click count; the other lets a tool clone a figure on creation. Which pair is correct?
questions.explanation: Per-click-count tool behavior is State (click states); cloning a prototype figure on creation is Prototype. (JHotDraw p.32, p.46)

questions.stem: A teammate labels a structure-only cleanup that adds a feature 'refactoring'. Why is the label wrong?

options.text: Refactoring must always add a feature
Refactoring changes structure WITHOUT changing behavior; adding a feature changes behavior, so it is not refactoring
Refactoring only applies to test code
Refactoring is the same as replacement

questions.explanation: Refactoring is strictly structure-change without behavior-change; adding a feature alters behavior and so is not refactoring. (SoftwareChange p.4)

questions.stem: A maintainer treats the classes from concept location as the FINAL list of classes to edit and skips dependency analysis. What did they skip, and what is the risk?

options.text: They skipped Initiation; the request was never prioritized
They skipped Verification; nothing can be tested
They skipped growing the impact set; dependent classes that also need changes may be missed
They skipped nothing; concept location already gives the full set

questions.explanation: Concept-location classes are only the initial impact set; Impact Analysis grows it by dependency analysis, and skipping that risks missing affected classes. (SoftwareChange p.8-9)

questions.stem: Reverse-identify the JHotDraw class: it extends AWT Panel, is the view, and is paired one-to-one with the application window. Which is it?
questions.explanation: StandardDrawingView (extends AWT Panel) is the view, paired 1—1 with DrawApplication. (JHotDraw p.5)
